current_band: 2-stability (north-star audit: SQLite connection pragmas now apply to every connection, not just the first)
last_item: Band-2 (stability) — NORTH-STAR AUDIT ITERATION (bands 0/1/1.5/2/2.5/3 all complete; only band 4 — cosmetic shadcn redo, mechanically blocked + rule-4/5 conflict — remains open, so an audit is the higher-value pick per the iter-51 note). Audited the THREE areas prior audits skipped (iter-44 = backup/auth/PG-lifecycle/loop; iter-48 = migration/scheduler/leaks/pooler-atomicity): the SQLite store layer, config loading + secret encryption, and the alert notifier delivery path (general-purpose subagent, source-traced). Config + notifier got a CLEAN bill: secrets are plaintext-at-rest by deliberate 0600-file design (no swallowed encryption), redaction is comprehensive (String/LogValue/GoString across all fmt verbs + slog), pgBackRest config written atomically 0600 with injection guards; notifier has a 15s client timeout + ctx-bound, one bad channel logs-and-presses-on (never blocks others or wedges the loop), no retry-hammer, no secret in error logs/URLs. FOUND + FIXED one MEDIUM 'never get stuck' store defect: the four connection pragmas (busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=on, synchronous=NORMAL, journal_mode=WAL) were applied ONCE via db.Exec on the pooled *sql.DB (store.go applyPragmas). With SetMaxOpenConns(1) the single conn normally persists, but database/sql can discard+reopen it (driver error / idle eviction) and a fresh conn then silently reverts the PER-CONNECTION pragmas. Load-bearing one is busy_timeout: reverting to 0 turns a transient lock into an immediate 'database is locked' surfaced to the operator instead of the intended 5s wait. (foreign_keys is currently inert — schema declares no FKs — but encoding it costs nothing + is correct for future columns; journal_mode=WAL is file-header-persistent so it survived regardless.) FIX: encode the pragmas into the DSN as _pragma=... query params via new buildDSN(path), which the modernc.org/sqlite v1.52.0 driver re-runs as PRAGMA ... on EVERY connection open — verified by reading the driver: applyQueryParams is called inside the per-connection open path (conn.go newConn -> openV2 -> applyQueryParams, driver sorts busy_timeout first). Removed applyPragmas; sql.Open is lazy so a bad-pragma error surfaces at first use, db.Close on the migrate() error path unchanged. Empty-path guard returns the DSN unchanged (driver only parses query params when '?' is not the first char). Reformatted connectionPragmas from 'PRAGMA x = y' strings to the 'x(y)' functional-pragma form the _pragma param expects. Tests (test-first, non-vacuous — PROVEN RED by neutering buildDSN to return the bare path: busy_timeout read back as 0): TestConnectionPragmasApplyToEveryConnection forces SetMaxIdleConns(0) so each query opens a brand-new connection, then asserts busy_timeout=5000 + foreign_keys=1 + journal_mode=wal on that fresh conn; TestBuildDSNEncodesPragmas pins path-preserved + each pragma encoded + empty-path passthrough. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): NO high-confidence findings — DSN parsing for plain/':memory:'/space-paths, non-regression on WAL + the removed db.Close path, secret-handling unchanged (no secret ever in the DSN), and test non-vacuousness all confirmed sound. Go gates green (gofmt/vet/test incl. the store package/build); web untouched. _prev = Band-3 (usability) — closed the LAST open band-3 item: the opt-in PgBouncer pooler now round-trips on↔off. It previously had no off switch (Enable/IsEnabled + status/enable only), so an operator who enabled it was stuck shelling in to undo it — 'safe optional overrides' implies reversibility. Added Manager.DisableNow (`systemctl disable --now pgbouncer`, idempotent inverse of EnableNow, internal/pgbouncer/service.go) + Manager.Disable(ctx, state) orchestrator (enable.go) which stops the service FIRST then persists EnabledConfigKey=disabledValue('false'). Ordering is the load-bearing safety choice (inverse of Enable's persist-last rule): a stop failure returns the error WITHOUT clearing the flag, so the panel never reports the pooler off while it is actually still running and accepting connections (recording off then failing to stop would be the more dangerous lie). New CSRF-gated POST /api/pooler/disable (handlePoolerDisable, no body — nothing to size when turning off) under requireAuth, so the band-1 TestEveryStateChangingEndpointRejectsCSRF route-walk auto-covers it; audited success/failure by code only. UI: red 'Disable connection pooler' button on the enabled view behind a tone='danger' ConfirmDialog stating exactly what stops (service down, no restart on reboot, apps pointed at the pooler fail to connect until repointed at Postgres) + reassurance (no PG restart, no data touched, re-enable anytime); plain (not typed-name) confirm because disabling is reversible. onEnabled→onChanged prop rename (it now covers both directions); new api client disablePooler(). Tests (test-first): manager TestDisable_StopsServiceThenPersistsOff / _ServiceStopFailureDoesNotPersist (the load-bearing safety property, RED on a persist-first impl: flag stays enabled when stop fails) / _IdempotentWhenAlreadyOff / _PersistFailureSurfaces / _RequiresRunnerAndState; handler TestPoolerDisable_RequiresAuth (401, flag untouched); web 3 tests (disable button only when on, confirm copy asserts each consequence, failed-disable keeps dialog open + no success signal). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): both Important findings applied pre-commit (tone='danger' on the confirm so the dialog button matches the danger framing; ConfDir=t.TempDir() on the nil-runner test to remove latent path fragility) — safety ordering, CSRF gating, and handler audit all confirmed sound. Go + web gates green; dist rebuilt + staged. BAND 3 NOW FULLY COMPLETE. _prev = Band-2 (stability) — north-star audit MEDIUM: ssh-less S3 migration no longer OOMs on a multi-GB dump. The ssh-less (shared-bucket) migration path buffers the WHOLE pg_dump in memory — ExportToSession does os.ReadFile -> PutObject([]byte) (orchestrator.go) and ImportFromSession does GetObject -> []byte -> WriteFile — so a multi-GB DB could OOM-kill the single binary mid-migration (direct-pull streams to a file and was unaffected). Took the MINIMAL INTERIM per the backlog (not the larger io.Reader/io.Writer interface change): added MaxSessionDumpBytes = 1 GiB + a shared errDumpTooLarge helper. EXPORT guard checks info.SizeBytes (the on-disk dump size pg_dump reports) immediately after Dump and BEFORE os.ReadFile, so an over-cap dump is never read into RAM/uploaded; the source session is moved to failed via failSession (already past the exporting transition). IMPORT guard checks sess.DumpSize (recorded by the exporter, which enforces the same ceiling) BEFORE GetObject pulls the object into a []byte. Over-cap -> CodeValidation error whose message points the operator at the direct-pull migration (streams to disk, no ceiling). KNOWN RESIDUAL (honestly documented in the import-side comment): the import guard TRUSTS the recorded DumpSize — a peer recording a small size but uploading a large object would still OOM; acceptable interim (both panels run trusted indiepg builds, per-session bucket short-lived); full fix needs a streaming GetObject. Tests (test-first, RED->GREEN, non-vacuous): TestExportToSession_dumpTooLargeRefusedBeforeUpload (refuses, no PutObject, never reaches PhaseUploading, session->failed, message contains direct-pull) and TestImportFromSession_dumpTooLargeRefusedBeforeDownload (refuses, no restore, never reaches PhaseDownloading, session->failed, message contains direct-pull). Proven RED before the guards landed (export still uploaded; import errored as CodeInternal not CodeValidation). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): ONE finding — my import-side comment inverted the threat direction (claimed a too-LARGE recorded size is the danger; the real residual is a too-SMALL recorded size) — fixed before commit; guard placement (truly before ReadFile/GetObject), failSession coverage on both StatusExporting/StatusImporting arms, and test invariants all confirmed sound. Go gate green (all packages); web untouched. _prev = Band-2 (stability) — north-star audit HIGH: migration workers can no longer hang forever on a stalled source. The three detached workers (runDirectJob/runExportJob/runImportWorker, internal/server/migrate_worker.go) ran on bare context.Background() with no deadline, and migrate.connArgs (engine.go) set no connect timeout — a source that accepts the TCP connection then stalls (firewall black-hole, overloaded host) made validateSource/pg_dump block indefinitely, leaving the migration stuck in `importing` forever with the work dir uncleaned until the next restart sweep. Direct-pull is the primary path and the source is arbitrary user input. FIX (2 parts): (1) PGCONNECT_TIMEOUT=10 added to the REMOTE branch of connArgs — bounds libpq's connect/auth phase only, never a running dump, so it can't abort a legit slow migration; a dead source now fails fast on connect. (2) every worker runs on workerContext()=context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), migrationJobTimeout=6h), a generous backstop for a stall beginning after connect; the orchestrator already threads ctx into every engine call so the deadline propagates + the work dir is cleaned on return. CRITICAL reviewer catch (finding #1): rec.Fail/rec.Succeed wrote through the SAME now-expired worker ctx, so on a timeout the SQLite write (QueryContext/ExecContext honour ctx) no-ops and the job stays wedged in `importing` — defeating the fix. Fixed at the persistence boundary in ONE place (covers the worker timeout arm AND the orchestrator's fail/failSession-from-a-stalled-dump): storeRecorder.Fail/Succeed persist on context.WithTimeout(context.WithoutCancel(ctx), 10s), matching the backup-manager detached-ctx convention. Tests (test-first + the reviewer regression, all non-vacuous): connArgs remote-has/local-lacks the timeout; orchestrator stalled-source (blocking Version) fails at the deadline with errors.Is(err, DeadlineExceeded) and never dumps; workerContext bounded + shrunk timeout fires; Fail persists `failed` status despite an already-cancelled ctx (RED on old direct-ctx code). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): blocking #1 fixed pre-commit; #2/#3 non-blocking (covered by the persistence-boundary test). Go gate green incl. -race; web untouched. _prev = Band-1.5 (data durability) — north-star AUDIT ITERATION that found+fixed a HIGH-severity migration defect (audit covered areas iter-44 skipped: migration, scheduler, goroutine/handle leaks, pooler atomicity). FOUND: whole-cluster migration aborts partway on the `postgres` maintenance DB. `engine.ListDatabases` (internal/migrate/engine.go) excluded only template0/template1, so the source's `postgres` DB entered `directCluster`'s per-database loop (orchestrator.go). Overwrite mode: `DropDatabase(target,"postgres")` runs `DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS postgres` while connected to `-d postgres` (DropDatabase always connects to -d postgres) → 'cannot drop the currently open database' → the cluster move ABORTS after earlier DBs were already dropped+restored (a half-migrated box). Non-overwrite mode: the restore's `CREATE DATABASE postgres` collides with the target's own maintenance DB. Every real cluster HAS a `postgres` DB, so this fired on essentially every cluster move; the existing tests only ever used appdb/analytics as sources so it was invisible to CI. FIX (test-first): added `'postgres'` to the SQL `NOT IN ('template0','template1','postgres')` exclusion in `engine.ListDatabases`. Its SOLE caller is the cluster loop (verified by grep: the panel's `GET /databases` uses a DIFFERENT `pg.ListDatabases` over the pgx pool), so `total := len(dbs)`, the dump/restore loop, AND the verify loop (orchestrator.go ~:271) all consistently exclude postgres with ONE change — no counter drift. Globals (pg_dumpall -g, replayed before per-db restores) carry roles/grants, so the maintenance DB holds no user data worth moving; a source that stored user tables in `postgres` is now silently skipped (a net improvement over crashing the whole move). Single-DB path (`directSingle`) is unaffected — it never calls ListDatabases, uses job.Source.Database directly, so an operator explicitly picking `postgres` still works. Test `TestListDatabases_excludesPostgresMaintenanceDB`: filtering is SQL-side (the FakeRunner can't execute SQL) so it asserts the query argv carries `NOT IN` + the quoted `'postgres'` literal (unambiguous vs the unquoted `-d postgres` connection arg); proven RED (old SQL has no quoted 'postgres') → GREEN. Updated both the impl and interface doc comments. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues — sole-caller confirmed, count-consistency confirmed, test non-vacuous, single-DB path isolated, silent-skip is a design improvement not a regression. THE OTHER AUDIT FINDINGS were appended to the backlog (NOT fixed this iter — rule 1, one item): (band 2, HIGH) migration workers run on bare context.Background() with no timeout + connArgs has no connect_timeout → a stalled source hangs the worker FOREVER (migration stuck in `importing`, work dir uncleaned until next restart sweep); (band 2, MEDIUM) ssh-less S3 export/import is `[]byte`-based → holds the whole dump in RAM → multi-GB DB can OOM-kill the binary mid-migration; (band 3, LOW/MED) no pooler Disable inverse (enable is irreversible from the UI). The scheduler (panic-recovered per-job; malformed cron rejected at Register; Start/Stop ctx-watcher released via icancel — no leak), background loop, interrupted-migration restart sweep, and notifier HTTP (timeouts + drainClose) were all confirmed SOLID.
_unused_prev3_band3ui: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 3/3 — React toggle on Settings (web/src/views/Pooler.tsx, rendered after DatabaseTuning in Settings.tsx). FEATURE COMPLETE; band 3 COMPLETE. Off by default. OFF view: plain-language explainer (when you don't need a pooler), previews loopback address (host:listen_port) + host-sized pool sizing labeled by effect (PoolSettingsTable), role picker filtered to non-superuser LOGIN roles (superusers connect directly; pool reserves conns for admin). Enable gated behind ConfirmDialog stating EXACTLY what happens first (installs pgbouncer pkg, starts service on host:listen_port, routes N named roles) + reassures NO Postgres restart, NO data touched. Enable button disabled until >=1 role picked AND status.pool != null (PG reachable — matches server 409 when Applied==nil). Client sends {roles} ONLY, never max_connections (server sizes pool; forged value can't widen it). ON view: ok callout + address + live pool sizing (or 'sizing unavailable' if pool null). New api client poolerStatus()/enablePooler() + 4 TS types (PoolRecommendation/PoolerStatus/PoolerEnableRequest/PoolerEnableResult) mirroring Go JSON. Tests Pooler.test.tsx (9): enabled address+sizing, enabled-PG-unreachable, disabled role filtering, button gated on selection, gated when PG unreachable, roles-loading shows spinner NOT empty state, no-app-roles empty state, confirm copy asserts each claim + sends {roles} + onEnabled called, failed-enable surfaces error + no success signal. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): fixed Important roles-loading race (flashed 'No app roles' before list arrived -> now threads rolesLoading + spinner) + minor state-order. _prev UI slice 2/3 = enable endpoint POST /api/pooler/enable
_unused_prev2: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 2/3 — enable endpoint POST /api/pooler/enable (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go handlePoolerEnable). Wires the already-built/tested pgbouncer.Manager.Enable orchestrator into HTTP. New pooler *pgbouncer.Manager field built in newServer over the shared OS runner (s.pg as VerifierSource, s.store as PoolerState). Under requireAuth → inherits CSRF gate for cookie flows; no exempt entry so band-1 TestEveryStateChangingEndpointRejectsCSRF route-walk auto-covers it. Input {roles, profile} ONLY — max_connections is NOT client-supplied; pool sized server-side from live s.pg.CurrentTuning().Applied.MaxConnections (security tie-break: forged value can't widen pool past what PG serves). Guard order cheap→costly: empty roles→400 (empty auth_file locks every app out), unknown profile→400 (ParseWorkloadProfile no silent mis-size), then Applied==nil (PG unreachable)→409 CodeConflict with NO side effect rather than guess-then-install. Audited enable_pooler success/failure by CODE only (never role names/verifiers; Enable itself logs role COUNT only). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: requires-auth(401,flag-stays-off), empty-roles-400, unknown-profile-400, refuses-when-PG-unreachable-409(flag-stays-off). Full side-effecting happy path NOT HTTP-tested by deliberate convention (backups/migrate handlers same: real OSRunner, test only guard/validation) — orchestration proven in pgbouncer/enable_test.go via fake runner. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no high-confidence findings. REMAINING: slice 3/3 React UI pooler toggle.
_unused_prev: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler UI slice 1/3 — read-only status endpoint GET /api/pooler (internal/server/handlers_pooler.go), behind requireAuth, returns {enabled, host, listen_port, pool}. enabled from pgbouncer.IsEnabled (config key pooler.enabled; absent=default-off); host/listen_port the loopback target same-box apps use (new exported pgbouncer.LoopbackHost/DefaultListenPort aliasing the unexported consts, one source of truth); pool the host-sized RecommendPool(max_connections, Mixed) sizing enabling would apply so the UI labels each setting by effect. Pool best-effort via s.pg.CurrentTuning — Postgres unreachable (Applied==nil) -> Pool nil, UI says computed-at-enable-time, never a guess. NEVER mutates host state; tuning-read error non-fatal so enabled still renders. Split the old single 'UI toggle' backlog item into 3 slices (this status endpoint done; future POST enable endpoint; React UI). Tests handlers_pooler_test.go: default-off, reflects-persisted-enable (reads same key Enable writes), requires-auth (401, no SPA leak). Dropped a premature unused pooler *pgbouncer.Manager Server field (YAGNI). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no critical/blocking; declined the one Important finding (test CurrentTuning-error branch) — currently unreachable, would need an injection seam nothing else uses.
_prev_last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler enable-flow slice 7 — backend ORCHESTRATOR. internal/pgbouncer/enable.go Manager.Enable(ctx, src VerifierSource, state PoolerState, p EnableParams) (EnableResult, error) wires the six already-built primitives into one opt-in entry point. OFF by default; idempotent/re-runnable. Order: InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers (src, satisfied by *pg.Manager) -> EnsureConfig -> EnsureUserlist -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/auth_file changed -> IsRunning verify -> persist enabled=true LAST (only once confirmed up, so the flag can't contradict a failed bring-up). Added IsEnabled (unset key=default-off) for the UI slice; PoolerState is a narrow GetConfig/SetConfig iface *store.Store satisfies (no store coupling into the package). SECURITY (reviewer-caught, fixed pre-commit): EnsureConfig BEFORE EnsureUserlist — the .ini marker guard is the flow's only ownership check, so the secret-adjacent auth_file is never written into a dir indiepg doesn't own; a foreign distro/operator pgbouncer.ini is a hard stop with no auth_file left behind. Logs role COUNT only, never names/verifiers. Tests enable_test.go via fake VerifierSource + in-memory PoolerState + temp confdir (10 Enable cases + IsEnabled). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): both findings applied (ordering swap + bring-up-failure test hardening). REMAINING: UI pooler toggle slice.
last_result: green: gofmt -l empty, go vet ./... ok, go test ./... -count=1 all packages pass (incl. internal/store), CGO_ENABLED=0 go build ./cmd/indiepg ok. Web untouched (Go-only change). Tree clean.
notes: ITER 52 was a NORTH-STAR AUDIT iteration that found + fixed one MEDIUM 'never get stuck' store defect (SQLite connection pragmas applied once on the pool instead of per-connection -> busy_timeout could silently revert to 0 on a reopened connection -> 'database is locked' instead of a 5s wait). Audited store + config + notifier (the areas iter-44 and iter-48 audits skipped); config (plaintext-at-rest by deliberate 0600 design, full redaction, atomic 0600 pgBackRest writes, injection guards) and notifier (15s timeout, ctx-bound, one-bad-channel-presses-on, no retry-hammer, no secret leak) both got a CLEAN bill. NEXT ITERATION: two known store follow-ups the audit flagged but did NOT fix (rule 1 = one item/iter), both LOW/latent, judge whether load-bearing before picking: (A) no schema versioning — migration is additive-only CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS with no PRAGMA user_version, so the FIRST future release that needs to ADD A COLUMN to an existing table will no-op silently and break queries for already-installed users (not a present-day defect — there's no such migration yet — but worth a user_version + ordered ALTER TABLE step BEFORE the first additive change is needed; could be pre-emptively scaffolded); (B) nothing else load-bearing surfaced. If neither A nor a fresh audit finds load-bearing work, the COMPLETE.md completion check becomes a genuine candidate — but each of the last several audits (iters 44/45/46/48/49/50/51/52) DID surface a real fix, so prefer one more audit pass over a different subsystem (e.g. the web API handler input-validation surface, the exec/runner layer, or identity/instance bootstrap) before declaring done. Band 4 (cosmetic shadcn UI redo) stays open but is BOTH mechanically blocked (sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only EROFS; installing the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide stack needs network) AND in tension with rule 5 (YAGNI/KISS — don't add deps the indie hacker didn't ask for) + rule 4 (security tie-break — widens supply-chain surface on a DB admin panel); the existing hand-CSS UI is lean + fully tested (67 web tests). --- HISTORICAL: ITER 51 closed the LAST open band-3 item (pooler disable). With bands 0/1/1.5/2/2.5/3 ALL complete, the ONLY remaining open backlog work is BAND 4 (cosmetic Tailwind+shadcn UI redo), which every prior iteration has judged to conflict with the non-negotiable rules — rule 5 (YAGNI/KISS: prefer hardening, don't add deps the indie hacker didn't ask for) and rule 4 (security tie-break: a DB admin panel pulling in the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide dep tree widens supply-chain surface) — AND which is mechanically blocked: installing the stack needs network + a writable ~/.npm cache, but the sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only (EROFS). The existing hand-CSS UI is already lean + fully tested (67 web tests). NEXT ITERATION should pick the higher-value of: (A) another north-star deep audit pass (audits ARE valid iterations; the iter-48 audit found 1 HIGH + 2 lower, all now fixed across iters 48-51 — migration postgres-DB abort, worker-hang-forever, S3 OOM cap, and now pooler disable), or (B) re-evaluate band 4 if its blockers/value change. Given band 4's rule-4/5 tension + mechanical block, (A) a fresh audit is the likely higher-value pick. The COMPLETE.md completion check is NOT yet warranted: a fresh audit could still surface load-bearing defects (each of the last several audits did). --- HISTORICAL: ITER 48 was a NORTH-STAR AUDIT iteration (bands 0-3 already complete; only band 4 — cosmetic shadcn redo, mechanically blocked + rule-4/5 conflict — remains open, so an audit is the higher-value pick per the iter-47 note). The audit (general-purpose subagent) covered the areas iter-44's audit SKIPPED: the DB migration feature, scheduler/cron robustness, goroutine/handle/connection leaks, and pooler enable atomicity. It found a HIGH-severity migration defect (whole-cluster migration aborts partway on the `postgres` maintenance DB) — FIXED this iter (see last_item). The OTHER findings were added to the backlog, NOT fixed (rule 1 = one item/iter): (band 2, HIGH) migration workers hang FOREVER on a stalled source (bare context.Background(), no connect_timeout) — likely the next-highest-value pick; (band 2, MEDIUM) ssh-less S3 migration holds the whole dump in RAM → OOM on multi-GB DBs; (band 3, LOW/MED) no pooler Disable inverse. Confirmed SOLID (no action): scheduler (per-job panic recovery, malformed-cron rejection, no ctx-watcher leak), background loop, interrupted-migration restart sweep, notifier HTTP. NEXT ITERATION should pick the band-2 'migration workers hang forever' item (HIGH, concrete fix: WithTimeout + PGCONNECT_TIMEOUT) — it's the highest open risk to 'never get stuck'. After the two band-2 migration-robustness items + the band-3 pooler-disable item, re-evaluate whether to do another audit pass or finally tackle band 4. --- HISTORICAL: ITER 47 closed the LAST open band-2.5 item — the audit-found LOW `Manager.IsRunning` wrapper-lie follow-up (now probes via confirmAcceptingConnections' SELECT 1 instead of the lying `systemctl is-active`). BAND 2.5 IS NOW FULLY COMPLETE. With bands 0/1/1.5/2/2.5/3 all done, the ONLY remaining open work is BAND 4 (cosmetic Tailwind+shadcn UI redo), which prior iterations judged to conflict with the non-negotiable rules — rule 5 (YAGNI/KISS: prefer hardening, don't add deps the indie hacker didn't ask for) and rule 4 (security tie-break: a DB admin panel pulling in the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide dep tree widens supply-chain surface) — and which is ALSO mechanically blocked: installing the stack needs network + a writable ~/.npm cache, but the sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only (EROFS). The existing hand-CSS UI is already lean + fully tested. NEXT ITERATION should pick the highest-value of: (A) another north-star audit pass (audits ARE valid iterations — the last one (iter 44) surfaced 1 critical + 2 medium + 1 low, all now fixed; a fresh deep audit may find more), or (B) re-evaluate band 4 (if pursued: scaffold Tailwind+shadcn ONE view at a time each with a test; web gate MUST run from web/ — repo-root npm hits read-only ~/.npm EROFS — AND installing deps needs the sandbox disabled; stage internal/server/web/dist with any web change so the tree stays clean). Given band 4's rule-4/5 tension + mechanical block, (A) a fresh audit is likely the higher-value pick until band 4's blockers are resolved or its value is reaffirmed. --- HISTORICAL (iter 44-46): iter 44 ran a north-star audit (1 CRITICAL self-heal wrapper-lie fixed iter 44, 2 MEDIUM silent-failure items fixed iter 45+46, 1 LOW IsRunning follow-up fixed THIS iter). All audit findings now closed. --- HISTORICAL (iter 44 context): ITER 44 was an AUDIT-FOUND REGRESSION FIX, not band-4 work. Bands 0-3 are complete; the ONLY remaining band is 4 (cosmetic Tailwind+shadcn UI redo). I judged band 4 to conflict with the non-negotiable rules — rule 5 YAGNI/KISS ('prefer hardening; don't add dependencies the indie hacker didn't ask for') and rule 4 security-tie-break (a DB admin panel pulling in the Tailwind/Radix/CVA/lucide dep tree widens the supply-chain surface), and the existing hand-CSS UI is already lean + fully tested. Also a hard mechanical blocker: installing the stack needs network + a writable ~/.npm cache, but the sandbox makes ~/.npm/_cacache read-only (EROFS). So instead of band 4 I ran a north-star audit (general-purpose subagent, 4 deep dives across backup/restore, auth/HTTP, PG lifecycle, background loop) which surfaced ONE critical load-bearing defect (the systemctl-wrapper-exit-0 lie in restartWithRollback — now FIXED this iteration) plus two MEDIUM silent-failure edges still OPEN (see below). NEXT ITERATION should pick the highest-value of: (A) the two audit-found MEDIUM silent-failure items [now in backlog under band 1.5/2.5], (B) the IsRunning wrapper-lie follow-up [band 2.5, LOW], or (C) re-evaluate band 4. AUDIT-FOUND OPEN ITEMS to add/prioritise: (1) backup-failed alert can go fully silent if the `fail` row insert ALSO fails — collector.go:77-91 derives LastBackupFailed ONLY from the newest backup_history row, and recordBackup (backup/manager.go ~:473) swallows insert errors; degrades to the 26h backup-stale warning. Fix: emit the alert from the in-memory backup outcome, independent of the store write. (2) alert/engine.go:104-112 — a single rule's persist failure does `return nil, err`, discarding every firing/recovery event already computed for OTHER rules that cycle (e.g. a disk-almost-full firing computed earlier in the loop). Fix: accumulate + persist independently, or append before persisting. Audit gave bands 1 (security), restore-clobber-safety, provisioning idempotency, and background-loop survival a CLEAN bill of health. --- BAND 4 (if pursued later): scaffold Tailwind+shadcn ONE view at a time, each with a test, no regressions; web gate MUST run from web/ (repo-root npm hits read-only ~/.npm EROFS) AND installing new deps needs the sandbox disabled (network + cache write). Stage internal/server/web/dist with any web change so the tree stays clean. --- HISTORICAL: BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 + 3 COMPLETE. Band 3 closed this iteration: the LAST open band-3 item was the PgBouncer pooler UI toggle (slice 3/3, web/src/views/Pooler.tsx) — the whole opt-in pooler feature is now done end to end (pure pool math -> config/userlist render -> atomic installers -> pg_authid verifier query -> service-lifecycle primitives -> Enable orchestrator -> GET /api/pooler status -> POST /api/pooler/enable -> React toggle). Band-3 provision-flow item (a) was DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served read-only by the tuning card). NEXT (band 4, UI redo with shadcn — migrate ONE view per iteration, each with a test, no regressions): (4a) scaffold Tailwind + shadcn into the Vite app without regressing existing views (config, base components, one pilot view migrated) — acceptance: build green, pilot view visually intact, test passes; then (4b) migrate remaining views one per iteration (Login, Dashboard, RolesDatabases, Query, Backups, Migrate, Alerts, Settings). CAUTION for band 4: the embedded SPA is served from internal/server/web/dist (committed); `cd web && npm run build` regenerates it (JS/CSS hashes change) — stage dist with the change so the tree stays clean. Web gate MUST run from web/ (repo-root npm hits a read-only ~/.npm cache, EROFS). --- HISTORICAL band-3 detail below. BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 COMPLETE; BAND 3 (usability) WAS IN PROGRESS. Provision-flow item (a) DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served by the tuning card). The ONLY remaining band-3 feature is the OPT-IN PgBouncer pooler, now being built incrementally like RecommendTuning was. PgBouncer slice 1 DONE iter 33: pure host-sized pool math internal/pgbouncer/pool.go (RecommendPool + SettingsMap, ports sm/DEFAULTS.md: avail=max_conn-5, default_pool=int(avail*util) floor20, min=/4 floor5, reserve=/5 floor5, max_client=default*multiplex, idle 300/600; safe defaults scram/transaction/DISCARD-ALL hardcoded). NEXT band-3 sub-items (in order): (i) DONE iter 34 — pgbouncer.ini render internal/pgbouncer/config.go RenderConfig; (ii) DONE iter 35 — config installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureConfig (atomic temp+rename, owner pgbouncer 0640, marker-guarded foreign->CodeConflict, deterministic no-op, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard); (iii-a) DONE iter 36 — SCRAM userlist render internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) (pure render of the `"user" "verifier"` auth_file: >=1 entry, SCRAM-only/no-downgrade, verifier+username injection guards, dup-refused, deterministic sort); (iii-b) DONE iter 37 — atomic auth_file installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureUserlist(ctx, []UserlistEntry) (renders via RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically: temp+rename, 0640 owner pgbouncer, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard, deterministic no-op; NO marker guard since the format can't carry one — ownership gated upstream by EnsureConfig's .ini marker, auth_file is a derived satellite; logs only user count; DRYed shared atomicInstall(path,data,mode) out of writeConfigFile); (iii-c) DONE iter 38 — pg_authid SCRAM verifier query internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err): the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed, read via the PRIVILEGED psql path (rolpassword is superuser-only) — logs nothing, ValidateIdentifier+QuoteLiteral names, missing-role NotFound, empty-rolpassword Validation (never dropped), de-dup, deterministic sort, verbatim pass-through (SCRAM gate stays in RenderUserlist); (iii-d) DONE iter 39 — service-lifecycle primitives internal/pgbouncer/service.go: InstallPackage (apt update+install -y pgbouncer, noninteractive, idempotent, update-fail-stops-install), EnableNow (systemctl enable --now, idempotent), Reload (systemctl reload/SIGHUP first — no client-conn drop — restart fallback; both-fail CodeExec; caller reloads ONLY when changed), IsRunning (is-active: reported-state→false, empty-stdout+runner-error→CodeExec so verify-after-start can't misread 'couldn't ask' as 'down'). NOT wired yet. (iii-e) <== NEXT orchestrator Enable(...): InstallPackage -> RoleVerifiers -> EnsureUserlist + EnsureConfig -> EnableNow -> Reload ONLY when config/userlist changed -> IsRunning verify; OFF by default; idempotent; persist enabled state; any restart follows sm safety conventions; (iv) UI toggle (off by default) showing the app connection string via the pooler + pool settings labeled by effect, with a confirm stating exactly what enabling does + vitest test. After these, band 3 is complete -> band 4 (UI redo, shadcn). DONE iter 32: destructive-confirm AUDIT + TESTS — all confirm sites already state consequence + irreversibility and gate destructive ops behind a typed exact-name match; was untested, so added web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.test.tsx (8 tests) locking: plain-language consequence shown, TypedConfirmDialog cannot fire onConfirm until exact name typed (also gated while busy). No component changes. Band-3 item (b) DONE. Test-only tip: web npm/npx from repo ROOT hits a read-only ~/.npm cache (EROFS) when it tries to fetch — run the web gate from web/ (npm run typecheck/build/test) or invoke web/node_modules/.bin/{vitest,tsc} directly with the project path. NEXT IS BAND 3 (usability): top open items — (a) provision flow shows computed best-defaults up front with each override labeled by effect, safe to accept blindly; (c) PgBouncer opt-in pooler toggle (DEFAULTS.md pool math, off by default, shows app connection string). NOTE the tuning UI just shipped is read-only/preview; if a real in-panel profile-switch is ever wanted it MUST funnel through ApplyTuning's restartWithRollback + a confirm dialog stating the brief restart (fits band-3 destructive-confirm item) — but it is NOT load-bearing for the north star, so only add it if usability work surfaces a genuine need. DONE iter 31: APPLY host-sized tuning UI SURFACE — read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning, internal/pg/tuning_status.go) returns detected RAM/CPU + live applied settings (from pg_settings, whole MB, Applied=nil best-effort when PG unreachable) + per-profile host-sized recommendation; 'Database tuning' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows applied settings + a per-profile PREVIEW labeled by effect, with copy making clear a non-default selection changes nothing and a real switch needs a restart (no restart-trigger surface). DONE iter 30: APPLY host-sized tuning REAL-PG INTEGRATION TEST (internal/pg/tuning_apply_integration_test.go, //go:build integration, INDIEPG_PG_BINDIR-gated). Proves ApplyTuning end-to-end on a throwaway PG14 cluster: settings land in pg_settings (bytes via unit col, max_connections exact), 2nd run no-op, an unstartable max_connections=1 rolls back via restartWithRollback with PG up -> CodeSafety. tuningTestRunner translates the exact `systemctl restart postgresql` -> pg_ctl restart. INTEGRATION-TEST GOTCHAS: force a deterministic boot failure with a CROSS-GUC constraint (max_connections=1), NOT an oversized memory GUC (overcommit makes it hang); and pg_ctl start/restart needs -l <logfile> or the daemonized postmaster holds the captured stdout pipe and cmd.Run hangs forever. ONLY REMAINING band-2.5 item: the ApplyTuning UI SURFACE — show the applied host-sized defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect (oltp/olap/mixed); vitest/RTL test; off-the-default profile clearly described before it changes sizing. After that, band 2.5 is complete -> band 3 (usability). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
